patch.value: The settings file can contain API keys and passwords. Each save must enforce directory 0700 and file 0600 on POSIX, including pre-existing paths, while remaining portable.
Settings saves do not repair permissive filesystem modes
Secure mode options only affect newly created settings paths; existing permissive paths can remain exposed after save.
Existing settings directory is repaired to 0700; existing or new settings file is 0600 after save; Windows behavior remains supported; tests use isolated temp paths
Current fs mkdir/write mode semantics and audit expectations are reviewed.
Create settings directory/file with permissive mode, call saveSettings, and inspect mode.
Mode is only specified for creation and does not necessarily repair existing paths.
Local API credentials may retain overly broad read permissions.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: CodeQL password-hash/logging alerts remain false positives because the API key is only SHA-256 fingerprinted for masking and never logged raw.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Use chmod only on non-Windows platforms and isolate permission helpers for testing.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Secure creation flags are insufficient when users can migrate or pre-create configuration paths.

patch.value: Isolated temporary-path tests prove permissive modes are repaired; the full test suite and platform-safe branches pass.
Existing and new settings paths are private after save.
Added private-directory and private-file write helpers that repair POSIX modes to 0700 and 0600 on every save while retaining Windows compatibility.
Private settings mode repair merged in PR #45

author: codex-auto-release

patch.value: Fixed settings saves so pre-existing files and directories regain restrictive POSIX modes

message: Align closed item title with shipped outcome for generated changelog
